2010-10-01.title: Standoff in Ecuador Ends With Leader’s Rescue

2010-10-28.abstract: The rules that apply to the rest of Afghanistan are often irrelevant in the Wakhan Corridor.

2010-10-29.title: Region Fouled by Trash Loses Faith in Berlusconi
2010-10-29.abstract: Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi of Italy is facing discontent as trash piles up on the streets of Naples.

2010-10-29.title: Don’t Aid Hariri Tribunal, Hezbollah Warns
2010-10-29.abstract: A demand puts new pressure on Lebanon’s fragile government.

2010-10-29.title: Obama Visit to Indonesia Will Include a Speech
2010-10-29.abstract: The president’s stay in Jakarta will give him a chance to present himself before an adoring public at a time when his popularity is down at home.

2010-10-29.title: U.S. Accuses Syria of Destabilizing Lebanon
2010-10-29.abstract: The American ambassador to the United Nations, Susan E. Rice, accused Damascus of working with Iran to arm Hezbollah with ever more potent weapons.

2010-10-29.title: Remembering Rabin, Some See His Legacy Fading
2010-10-29.abstract: Fifteen years after his assassination, Prime Minister Yitzhak Rabin of Israel is still honored as a man, but his legacy as a peacemaker is debated by the left and right.

2010-10-30.title: Suicide Bomber Kills 25 at Cafe in Iraq’s Diyala Province
2010-10-30.abstract: The attack, in the eastern town of Balad Ruz, was the deadliest in a month and stirred fears that insurgents were taking advantage of the political deadlock.

2010-10-30.title: U.S. Hears Echo of Japan’s Woes
2010-10-30.abstract: The confidence U.S. policy makers had that the country could avoid the economic fate of Japan is now waning.

2010-10-30.title: A Saint, and an Inquiry Into the Sins of His Brothers
2010-10-30.abstract: Amid the celebrations over Brother André’s canonization looms the issue of sexual abuse at his order.

2010-10-30.title: Yemen Emerges as Base for Qaeda Attacks on U.S.
2010-10-30.abstract: American citizens appear to be helping the Yemen branch of Al Qaeda take aim at the United States.

2010-10-30.title: Lebanon: International Tribunal Says Hezbollah Tried to Obstruct Justice in Prime Minister’s Killing
2010-10-30.abstract: The international tribunal investigating the 2005 killing of the former Lebanese prime minister, Rafik Hariri, said that calls to boycott cooperation with the tribunal constituted “a deliberate attempt to obstruct justice.”

2010-10-31.title: Saudi Help in Package Plot Is Part of Security Shift
2010-10-31.abstract: Western officials say the Saudis’ own experience with jihadists has helped them develop powerful surveillance tools and a broad network of informers.

2010-10-31.title: Bomber Wounds 32 in Attack in Istanbul
2010-10-31.abstract: The attack on Sunday morning was aimed at a police unit in the busiest local and tourist district in Istanbul.

2010-10-31.title: Bomb Plot Shows Key Role Played by Intelligence
2010-10-31.abstract: The foiling of the package plot was a sobering reminder that quick response to timely intelligence rules the day.

2010-10-31.title: Iraqi Forces Storm a Church With Hostages in a Day of Bloodshed
2010-10-31.abstract: A government source said that the police had arrested eight gunmen believed to be affiliated with the Islamic State of Iraq, a militant organization connected to Al Qaeda in Mesopotamia.

2010-10-31.title: Somali-American Becomes Prime Minister of Somalia
2010-10-31.abstract: Mohamed Abdullahi Mohamed will take over the Western-backed transitional government, which controls just a few blocks of the capital and is struggling for survival amid a sea of Islamist insurgents.
